hero.title: Vietnam's first airport operations advisory.
hero.subtitle: We make international standards deliverable in Vietnam — licensing, operations and workforce, for the country's new wave of private airports.

airports.label: new private airports expected in Vietnam within five years

law.label: new Civil Aviation Law 130/2025 in force — a regulatory framework with no precedent

firms.label: domestic advisory firms focused on airport operations — until now

theGap.kicker: Airport operations consulting in Vietnam — from licensing to opening day.
theGap.heading: Vietnam builds airports. Nobody has been advising on how to run them.
theGap.p1: For two decades, Vietnamese airport projects have had world-class design consultants. What they have never had is a domestic advisor for the harder half of the problem — turning a finished terminal into a licensed, staffed, smoothly running airport. That knowledge has lived inside one state operator, and in the careers of the people who built the system.
theGap.p2: A wave of private airports is now arriving faster than that expertise can be rebuilt from scratch. Avio Group exists to close the gap — pairing the people who ran Vietnamese aviation with the international standards these new airports are being held to.

regulatory.title: Regulatory & Licensing
regulatory.summary: Airport-opening dossiers, aerodrome certification and structured engagement with the Vietnamese authorities under the 2025 Law.
regulatory.inPractice: In practice: preparing the airport-opening dossier and carrying it through the authorities under Law 130/2025.

operational.title: Operational Delivery
operational.summary: Readiness planning, trials, documentation and embedded bilingual teams that take an airport from built to flying.
operational.inPractice: In practice: writing the operations manuals and running the trials that get an airport to opening day.

workforce.title: Workforce & Capability
workforce.summary: Recruitment pipelines and certified training for the hundreds of operational staff every new airport needs.
workforce.inPractice: In practice: recruiting and certifying the hundreds of staff a new airport needs, through the national training system.

audiences.heading: Built for three kinds of partner.

internationalOperators.title: International operators & consultancies
internationalOperators.body: We are your local delivery layer — regulatory intelligence, embedded operations teams and workforce insight, at a Vietnamese cost base.

investors.title: Airport investors & developers
investors.body: We take licensing, readiness and staffing off your critical path, with fees tied to the milestones that matter.

financial.title: Financial institutions
financial.body: We assess the operational and licensing risk behind an airport loan — the half of the due diligence a financial model cannot cover.

whyNow.heading: Why this matters now.
whyNow.body: Vietnam's new Civil Aviation Law took effect on 1 July 2026, resetting how airports are certified and operated. At the same moment, a pipeline of private airports — Phu Quoc's expansion, Phan Thiet, Con Dao, Rach Gia and more — is moving from plan to construction to opening, much of it against public deadlines. The next five years will be decided by execution on the ground, not by design on paper. That is the work we do.

advisoryBench.body: Backed by a senior advisory board drawn from the leadership of Vietnam's aviation system — airport operations, ground services, civil aviation administration, construction quality and aviation training.
advisoryBench.subline: Advisor profiles available to partners under NDA.

ctaBand.text: Building or backing an airport in Vietnam? Let's talk.

experts.lead: A collective of the industry's most senior experts — regulators, operators and investors on one team.

intro.title: Introduction
intro.p1: Avio Group Company Limited ("Avio Group", "we", "us") is an airport operations advisory registered in Vietnam and identified by D-U-N-S Number 626314981. We operate the corporate website at aviogroup.eco.
intro.p2: This policy explains what information we collect through this website, how we use it, and the choices you have. It covers the website only; client engagements are governed by the separate agreements we sign with each partner.
intro.p3: If you have any questions about this policy, contact us at contact@aviogroup.eco.

collect.title: Information we collect
collect.p1: When you use our contact form we collect the name, organisation, role, email address and message you provide. When you join the market-report waitlist we collect your email address.
collect.p2: We collect limited technical information automatically, such as approximate region, browser type and pages viewed, to keep the site secure and understand how it is used.
collect.p3: We do not ask for, or intentionally collect, sensitive personal data through this website.

use.title: How we use information
use.p1: We use the details you submit to respond to your enquiry, to send you the market report you requested, and to keep a record of our correspondence.
use.p2: We use technical information to operate, secure and improve the website.
use.p3: We do not sell your information, and we do not use it for third-party advertising.

share.title: How we share information
share.p1: We share information with service providers who help us run the website and communicate with you — for example hosting, database (Firestore) and rate-limiting (Upstash) providers — under confidentiality obligations.
share.p2: We may disclose information where required by law or to protect our rights, and in connection with a corporate transaction, subject to continued protection.
share.p3: We do not otherwise share your information with third parties for their own purposes.

security.title: Data security
security.p1: We rely on reputable infrastructure providers that encrypt data in transit and at rest, and we limit access to the information submitted through this website.
security.p2: No method of transmission or storage is perfectly secure, but we take reasonable measures appropriate to the limited data this website handles.
security.p3: If you believe your interaction with this website is no longer secure, please contact us at contact@aviogroup.eco.

retention.title: Data retention
retention.p1: We keep enquiry and waitlist information for as long as needed to respond to you, to send the requested report, and to meet legal or record-keeping obligations.
retention.p2: You may ask us to delete the information you submitted at any time, and we will do so unless we are required to retain it.
retention.p3: Aggregated, non-identifying analytics may be kept for longer, as they are not linked to an identifiable person.

rights.title: Your rights
rights.p1: Depending on your location, you may have rights to access, correct, delete or port your personal data, and to object to or restrict certain processing.
rights.p2: You may also have the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority.
rights.p3: To exercise any of these rights, email contact@aviogroup.eco; we will respond within the time required by applicable law.

children.title: Children's privacy
children.p1: This is a business-to-business website directed at aviation firms, investors and authorities. It is not directed to children.
children.p2: We do not knowingly collect personal information from children.
children.p3: If you believe a child has provided information through this website, contact us and we will delete it.

transfers.title: International data transfers
transfers.p1: We are based in Vietnam and serve international partners. Information you submit may be processed outside your country by our service providers.
transfers.p2: Where data is transferred across borders, we rely on providers that offer recognised safeguards such as standard contractual clauses.
transfers.p3: Contact us if you have questions about where your information is processed.

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.p1: We use only the minimal cookies and local storage needed for the website to function, such as remembering your language and theme preference.
cookies.p2: We do not use third-party advertising cookies on this website.
cookies.p3: You can control cookies through your browser settings; disabling essential cookies may affect how the site works.

changes.title: Changes to this policy
changes.p1: We may update this policy to reflect changes to the website or legal requirements.
changes.p2: When we make material changes, we will update the "Last updated" date above.
changes.p3: Your continued use of the website after an update means you accept the revised policy.

contact.title: Contact us
contact.p1: For any privacy question or request, email contact@aviogroup.eco.
contact.p2: Postal correspondence: Avio Group Company Limited, Quang Ninh, Vietnam.
contact.p3: We aim to acknowledge privacy enquiries within a reasonable time.

acceptance.title: 1. Acceptance of these terms
acceptance.p1: These terms govern your use of the website operated by Avio Group Company Limited ("Avio Group") at aviogroup.eco.
acceptance.p2: By using the website, you agree to these terms. If you do not agree, please do not use the website.
acceptance.p3: If you use the website on behalf of an organisation, you confirm you are authorised to accept these terms for it.

services.title: 2. About this website
services.p1: This website provides general information about Avio Group's airport operations advisory services in Vietnam.
services.p2: The website is informational. It does not itself create any advisory, consulting or contractual relationship; any engagement is governed by a separate written agreement.
services.p3: We may change, suspend or discontinue parts of the website at any time.

accounts.title: 3. Your submissions
accounts.p1: When you submit information through our forms, you agree to provide accurate details and not to submit unlawful, misleading or harmful content.
accounts.p2: You are responsible for the content you submit and for ensuring you may share any information you provide about others.
accounts.p3: We may decline to respond to, or may delete, submissions that appear abusive, fraudulent or automated.

acceptableUse.title: 4. Acceptable use
acceptableUse.p1: You agree not to misuse the website, including by probing or scanning it for vulnerabilities, interfering with its operation, or introducing malware.
acceptableUse.p2: You may not scrape or harvest data, circumvent rate limits, or impersonate Avio Group or others.
acceptableUse.p3: We may investigate misuse and cooperate with authorities where the law requires.

ip.title: 5. Intellectual property
ip.p1: Avio Group and its licensors own the content, branding and materials on this website.
ip.p2: You may view and share the content for your own informational, non-commercial use, keeping any attribution intact.
ip.p3: You may not otherwise copy, reproduce or use our trademarks or materials without our written permission.

noAdvice.title: 6. No professional advice
noAdvice.disclaimerBold: The information on this website is general in nature and is not regulatory, legal, financial or operational advice for any specific airport, project or situation. Aviation licensing and operations depend on the facts, the jurisdiction and current regulation, including Vietnam's Civil Aviation Law 130/2025. Do not act on the basis of website content alone; obtain advice tailored to your circumstances under a formal engagement before making decisions.
noAdvice.p2: Any statements about outcomes, timelines or the market are for general context and are not guarantees.
noAdvice.p3: Avio Group is not responsible for decisions taken solely in reliance on this website.

liability.title: 7. Disclaimers and limitation of liability
liability.p1: The website is provided "as is" and "as available", without warranties of any kind to the maximum extent permitted by law.
liability.p2: To the maximum extent permitted by law, Avio Group is not liable for indirect, incidental or consequential damages arising from your use of the website.
liability.p3: Nothing in these terms limits liability that cannot be limited under applicable law.

termination.title: 8. Suspension
termination.p1: We may suspend or restrict access to the website, in whole or in part, where necessary to protect the website, our rights or other users.
termination.p2: You may stop using the website at any time.
termination.p3: Provisions that by their nature should survive — including intellectual property, disclaimers and governing law — continue to apply.

changes.title: 9. Changes to these terms
changes.p1: We may update these terms to reflect changes to the website or legal requirements.
changes.p2: We will update the "Last updated" date when we do, and material changes take effect when posted.
changes.p3: Your continued use of the website after an update means you accept the revised terms.

law.title: 10. Governing law
law.p1: These terms are governed by the laws of Vietnam, without regard to conflict-of-law rules.
law.p2: The competent courts of Vietnam have jurisdiction, subject to any mandatory rights you have where you reside.
law.p3: If any provision is found unenforceable, the remaining provisions continue in effect.

contact.title: 11. Contact
contact.p2: Avio Group Company Limited, Quang Ninh, Vietnam. D-U-N-S 626314981.
contact.p3: We publish our corporate verification details to help partners confirm our identity.

regulatory.intro: Opening an airport in Vietnam is a documentary process before it is an operational one. Every terminal, runway and security procedure has to be evidenced in an airport-opening dossier and defended through the authorities — now under the 2025 Civil Aviation Law, a framework with almost no precedent in practice. We prepare that dossier, manage the certification process, and stand between the project and the regulator until the licence is granted.
regulatory.typicalEngagement: Typically begins with a gap assessment of the current dossier against Law 130/2025.
regulatory.whoFor: International firms delivering mandates in Vietnam, and investors preparing a new airport for opening.

operational.intro: A finished terminal is not a functioning airport. Between construction and first flight sits the work that decides whether opening day holds — operational readiness planning, the manuals and procedures that govern every process, and trials that rehearse the airport under real conditions. We build that operational layer and, where useful, embed bilingual teams alongside the owner through to opening.
deliverables: Peak-season resource modelling and multi-airport staffing strategies
operational.typicalEngagement: Typically begins with an operational readiness assessment against the opening timeline.
operational.whoFor: Airport owners approaching opening day — or a peak season they cannot afford to miss.

workforce.intro: A new airport in a province that has never had one needs hundreds of certified staff on opening day, with no local pool to draw them from. Building that workforce means designing recruitment from zero, then training and certifying operational roles through the national aviation training system. We plan the staffing, run the pipeline, and transfer capability to the owner's own teams so the airport can run itself.
deliverables: Recruitment pipeline design for new airports
Certified training programme delivery through the national aviation training system
Capability transfer for owner's operations teams
workforce.typicalEngagement: Typically begins with a staffing model and recruitment plan for the target opening date.
workforce.whoFor: New airports that must build a certified operational workforce from zero — in provinces that have never had one.

TeamPage.framing: Between them, our advisors have licensed airports, run them through record peak seasons, trained the national workforce, and written the rules the industry runs on.

InsightsPage.intro: Coming this autumn: "Vietnam Private Airports — The Operations Gap", a joint market report with an international aviation research partner. Leave your email to receive it on release.

reportCovers.heading: The report will cover:
items: The private-airport pipeline and what each project needs to reach operation
What the 2025 Civil Aviation Law changes for airport certification and operations
The operational talent gap — and how new airports are staffing against it
Where international operators, investors and lenders fit in the new landscape
